MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Katie McClelland-Glenn, Esq., ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party National Bulk Carriers, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Meredith Gordon, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT NATIONAL BULK CARRIERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant National Bulk Carriers, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their causes of action against defendant. The limited number of special interrogatories that defendant submitted with its moving papers, which were not identical as to each plaintiff, are neither all facts interrogatories nor the equivalent of all facts interrogatories, neither were they asking for all witnesses and documents supporting those facts. Further, defendant only attached plaintiffs' responses to special interrogatory no. 1 and no more, so defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs' responses to any interrogatories other than no. 1 were factually devoid. (See Exhibits G and J.) Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR # 5193 = (503/TLJ)
